Recovery run emitted: (1) exactly 1 new transitions row carrying the original ts but the CURRENT run_id and a note `"recovered APPROVED→IN_PROGRESS from interrupted run run-2026-04-22T08:00:00Z-b3c4 (interrupt-during-persist)"`; (2) 1 matching changelog bullet appended; (3) lock released (run.lock absent / 0-byte); (4) all 6 seed items preserved; (5) prior-day 2026-04-20 reports byte-identical to seed. Step 7.5 passed with 0 hard/0 soft violations including F003-specific I1..I5: (I1) bidirectional history↔transitions integrity on (id, from, to, ts); (I2) no item deletion — output ids ⊇ seed ids, count==6; (I3) exactly 1 new transitions row, target=SEC-0003, from=APPROVED→to=IN_PROGRESS, note cites interrupted run; (I4) every seed reports/ file byte-identical in output; (I5) state/locks/run.lock absent or 0-byte. Fault-injection harness (fault_inject.py) independently confirmed all 9 drift scenarios trip the expected invariant (missing row→I1, ghost row→I1(bis), dropped item→I2, zero-new/wrong-note/wrong-from-to→I3, mutated prior-day→I4, non-empty lock→I5).
